system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types:
4
(
pureMixture
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture
multiComponentMixture
valueMultiComponentMixture
)
```

This indicates that in **OpenFOAM 10** (as seen from the path `/OpenFOAM-10/`), the mixture type `reactingMixture` is **no longer supported**. The Foundation version (OpenFOAM 10) restructured how reacting mixtures work — the old-style `reactingMixture` has been replaced.

## Root Cause

Looking at the `physicalProperties` file, the `mixture` entry is set to `reactingMixture`, which doesn't exist in OF10. Additionally, the `thermo.compressibleGas` file also references `reactingMixture`.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Fix `constant/physicalProperties`

Change the `mixture` type from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}
```

Also update the `foamChemistryThermoFile` path if needed (it should point to `<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas`).

### 2. Fix `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Similarly, change the `mixture` type in this file from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}
```

### 3. Update `constant/combustionProperties`

Ensure the `combustionModel` is set to `laminar;` (this appears correct already).

### Summary

The key fix across **both** `physicalProperties` and `thermo.compressibleGas` files is changing `mixture reactingsMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`. This is the only difference between these two files — they appear nearly identical, so both need the same correction. In OpenFOAM 10, the reacting mixture framework was refactored, and `multiComponentMixture` is now the appropriate choice when using a chemistry file (`reactions`) and species thermodynamic data.
</Review_Analysis>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

I have modified the files according to your previous suggestions. If the error persists, please provide further guidance. Make sure your suggestions adhere to user requirements and do not contradict it. Also, please consider the previous attempts and try a different approach.
